Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Change "Mina's counter-offer email delivering the 10% Preferred Partner Program with three service sweeteners (April 19)" to "(April 18)" or rephrase to "Mina's counter-offer email (April 18) and final hold email (April 19),both reinforcing the 10% Preferred Partner Program with three service sweeteners." This would bring OE Accuracy to 5.
OE 2: Search emails with a query related to "Kevin Tran" or "Danielle Osei" or "Vectral relocation" (or similar) using search_emails to find the relocation correspondence for both employees. The agent should discover that Kevin Tran's relocation (Portland → Denver) was completed successfully with move dates April 19–20, coordinated by Javier Morales via Heartland Movers. Danielle Osei's original relocation (Phoenix → Denver) was cancelled due to budget reallocation. A newer set of three Vectral April relocations (Priya Suresh, Terrence Nakamura, Camille Oduya) is currently active.
OE 3: Search Airtable relocation records for Vectral employees using airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations") to verify the status of Kevin Tran's and Danielle Osei's moves. The agent should find: Kevin Tran Status: Completed, Origin: Portland, OR, Destination: Denver, CO, Coordinator: Javier Morales. Danielle Osei Status: Completed (original record) or Cancelled (the rec_reloc_osei record). No complaints, delays, or issues are documented in the relocation records themselves for these two moves.
OE 4: Search Slack messages with a query related to "Kevin Tran" or "Danielle Osei" or "Vectral relocation" (or similar) using conversations_search_messages to check for any internal complaints, delays, or issues mentioned about these relocations. The agent should find Mina's post in #customer-engagement (C002) noting the two Denver relocations for Vectral with Javier assigned. No complaints or issues are found in Slack for these specific relocations.
OE 5: Search emails with a query related to "ExpenseBot" or "auto-categorizer" or "expense rejection" or "false rejection" (or similar) using search_emails to investigate the status of the expense auto-categorizer issue on the Vectral account. The agent should discover: (a) Danielle Osei's $475 ergonomic chair was auto-rejected by ExpenseBot due to a stale $400 default limit but was manually approved by Mina; (b) Kevin Tran's $185 internet setup fee was also auto-rejected and manually approved; (c) Three additional Vectral employees (Maria Santos $550 standing desk, James Okafor $82 ergonomic keyboard tray, Lisa Park $130 mesh WiFi system) had expenses auto-rejected and are still marked as "Rejected" indicating not all false rejections have been corrected; (d) Elena paused the ExpenseBot rollout pending a full audit.
OE 6: Search Airtable stipend transaction records using airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Stipend Transactions") with a filter for Vectral Systems to verify whether all ExpenseBot false rejections have been resolved. The agent should find that Maria Santos ($550 standing desk), James Okafor ($82 ergonomic keyboard tray), and Lisa Park ($130 mesh WiFi system) still have Approval Status of "Rejected" with review notes saying "Pending manual correction." This indicates the ExpenseBot issue is NOT fully resolved it is still a liability Rachel could bring up.
OE 7: Search emails with a query related to "Simone Richter" or "apartment" or "studio" or "BrightLoop" (or similar) using search_emails to find the status of Simone Richter's apartment situation. The agent should discover: Simone emailed Mina on approximately April 8 reporting that her UrbanNest placement was a studio instead of the promised one-bedroom. Mina replied on April 9 promising to get back by end of week but never followed up. The complaint has been sitting unanswered. Mina escalated this internally to Chloe and David, and UrbanNest (Carmen Reyes) has been contacted to investigate the discrepancy, but no resolution has been communicated back to Simone.
OE 8: Search emails with a query related to "Marcus Webb" or "Road Runner" or "Honda Civic" or "vehicle delay" (or similar) using search_emails to find the status of Marcus Webb's car shipment. The agent should discover: Road Runner Auto Transport sent a delay notice on April 11 stating Marcus's 2019 Honda Civic was stuck at their Indianapolis transfer hub due to a driver shortage, with a revised delivery window of April 18–20. Marcus sent two follow-up emails asking for a real ETA with no response. Blessing Okafor acknowledged the failure as hers (she never acted on the April 11 notice). Julian Brooks eventually stepped in and sent Marcus an apology, promising a fresh status pull from the carrier.
OE 9: Search Slack messages with a query related to "BrightLoop" or "Simone" or "Marcus Webb" (or similar) using conversations_search_messages to find internal discussions about the BrightLoop service gaps. The agent should find Mina's post in #customer-engagement (C002) flagging the two active BrightLoop failures (Simone's apartment misplacement and Marcus's car delay) and referencing Tessa Moreno's expansion note. A more detailed escalation naming the six new May relocations (Oliver Bancroft, Priya Naidu, Jonathan Marsh, Leah Kwon, Daniel Okafor, Sofia Reyes) was posted in #executive (C004).
OE 10: Look up the Vectral Systems company record using crm_search_companies with name "Vectral" (or similar) to find the account details, deal history, and engagement notes. The agent should discover company ID: company_vectral, the Q3 renewal deal (deal_vectral_q3_renewal_risk, dealstage: "at_risk"), and the engagement notes documenting the full negotiation timeline and counter-offer status.
OE 11: Look up the BrightLoop Analytics company record using crm_search_companies with name "BrightLoop" (or similar) to find the account details and engagement history. The agent should discover company ID: company_brightloop, the April 2026 relocations deal (deal_brightloop_apr2026, amount: $11,350, dealstage: closedwon for Simone Richter + Marcus Webb), and the engagement notes documenting the two active relocations.
OE 12: Look up Rachel Whitfield's contact details using crm_search_contacts with full_name "Rachel Whitfield" (or similar), or contacts_search_contacts with query "Rachel Whitfield" (or similar), to retrieve her verified email address rachel.whitfield@vectralsystems.com before sending the check-in email.
OE 13: Look up Tessa Moreno's contact details using crm_search_contacts with full_name "Tessa Moreno" (or similar), or contacts_search_contacts with query "Tessa Moreno" (or similar), to retrieve her verified email address tessa.moreno@brightloopanalytics.com before sending the email.
OE 14: Update the CRM with notes on where each BrightLoop issue stands using crm_create_engagement. The engagement should cover: (a) Simone Richter's apartment complaint UrbanNest placed her in a studio instead of a 1BR, complaint received April 8, Mina's follow-up missed, currently being investigated with UrbanNest; (b) Marcus Webb's vehicle delay 2019 Honda Civic stuck at Road Runner's Indianapolis hub since April 11, revised delivery window April 18–20, two follow-ups from Marcus went unanswered, Julian has now stepped in with an apology and is obtaining a fresh ETA. The engagement should be associated with BrightLoop (company_id: company_brightloop).
OE 15: Send a check-in email to Rachel Whitfield (via send_email) from mina.hashimoto@moveops.com to rachel.whitfield@vectralsystems.com. The email content should reinforce the value delivered on recent Vectral relocations (Kevin Tran's and Danielle Osei's completed moves, plus the current April wave of Priya Suresh, Terrence Nakamura, and Camille Oduya), and gently nudge on the renewal timeline without sounding desperate referencing the Preferred Partner Program and the service enhancements offered (dedicated onboarding coordinator, 48-hour response SLA, quarterly account reviews).
OE 16: Send an email to Tessa Moreno at BrightLoop (via send_email) from mina.hashimoto@moveops.com to tessa.moreno@brightloopanalytics.com. The email content should acknowledge the Simone Richter and Marcus Webb situations and provide a concrete update on resolution: (a) Simone's apartment issue is being investigated with UrbanNest to determine how a studio was delivered instead of the confirmed 1BR; (b) Marcus's Honda Civic shipment was delayed at Road Runner's Indianapolis hub and the team is actively working to get a firm delivery date.
OE 17: Log the Rachel Whitfield outreach touch in the CRM using crm_create_engagement. The engagement should document the check-in email sent to Rachel regarding the Vectral renewal, referencing the Preferred Partner Program and renewal follow-up. The engagement should be associated with Vectral Systems (company_id: company_vectral) and/or Rachel Whitfield's contact record (contact_id: contact_vectral_hr).
OE 18: Log the Tessa Moreno outreach touch in the CRM using crm_create_engagement. The engagement should document the email sent to Tessa regarding the Simone and Marcus situations at BrightLoop, referencing the concrete updates on resolution. The engagement should be associated with BrightLoop Analytics (company_id: company_brightloop) and/or Tessa Moreno's contact record (contact_id: contact_brightloop_hr).
OE 19: Post a summary message in the #customer-engagement Slack channel (channel_id: C002) using conversations_add_message. The message should flag two key issues for David Chen before the Monday sync: (a) Vectral renewal silence Rachel Whitfield has not responded since the board met on April 23 about the 10% Preferred Partner counter-offer, creating uncertainty about whether Vectral will renew; (b) BrightLoop service gaps Simone Richter's apartment was a studio instead of a 1BR (complaint unanswered for weeks), Marcus Webb's Honda Civic has been stuck in Indianapolis since April 11 with no concrete ETA communicated to the employee, and these unresolved issues risk the account relationship ahead of six new May relocations from Tessa Moreno.
OE 20: Search Linear issues related to the ExpenseBot or expense auto-categorizer using linear_list_issues with a query related to "ExpenseBot" or "expense" or "auto-categorizer" (or similar) to check whether Samira's engineering team has confirmed the false rejection issue is fully resolved. The agent should find issues under the proj_expense_autocategorizer project, including the launch readiness checklist (issue 219) and the stipend duplicate detection task (issue 220). The engineering status and whether a fix has been deployed helps determine if the issue is still a liability for the Vectral relationship.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

✗Run #5The agent incorrectly reported Danielle Osei's relocation as 'completed successfully' and 'ops clean,' stating the issues were expense-related. The correct finding from the universe data is that her Phoenix-to-Denver relocation was cancelled due to budget reallocation. The agent fabricated a successful completion. FAIL.
✗Run #6The agent incorrectly reported that Danielle Osei's relocation was completed successfully. The Airtable data shows two records for Danielle Osei: one Cancelled record (RELOC-2026-010, cancelled due to budget reallocation) and one Completed record (the recent April 2026 move). The agent missed the original cancelled relocation and reported both as completed on schedule.
